安尼捷比出番黎 應該護到級

老莫之前 訪問有講過 佢需要一個大隻前鋒係前面hold到波 俾派拿/贊仔後上，因為隊波 無以前愛華頓用波能力 由後打上去

之前11月有捷比 贏到果幾場好關鍵

利申 愛迷

TEAM NEWS

Everton striker Romelu Lukaku is available to face Sunderland following a minor calf problem.

James McCarthy and Kevin Mirallas return after recovering from hamstring and groin injuries respectively.

Sunderland goalkeeper Jordan Pickford is fit again after a knee problem and could make his first appearance since Boxing Day in place of Vito Mannone.

Jan Kirchhoff is back in training but will not be involved, while Steven Pienaar is doubtful with a calf injury.

Papy Djilobodji serves the last match of his suspension.

MATCH FACTS

Head-to-head

Everton have lost two of their last three homes games against Sunderland in the league, although they did win 6-2 in this fixture last season.
There have been 14 goals between these sides in their last three meetings, with the Toffees winning twice and Sunderland once.
An Everton player scored a hat-trick in two of those clashes - Arouna Kone at Goodison Park last season (6-2) and Romelu Lukaku in the reverse of this fixture in September (3-0).


Everton

Everton are unbeaten in eight league games (W5, D3) and have kept a clean sheet in five of those matches.
Their only league defeat in the last 15 home matches was against Liverpool on 19 December (W9, D5).
They have earned 25 points at Goodison Park this season, already two more than in the whole of 2015-16.
Ronald Koeman's side have amassed 14 points in 2017; it's a joint-league high with Tottenham, who have played a game more since the turn of the year.
Romelu Lukaku is one goal away from equalling Duncan Ferguson's record of 60 as the top Premier League scorer for Everton. He needs two goals to equal his highest tally of 18 in a single league campaign, set last season.
Leighton Baines could make his 300th Premier League appearance for Everton, becoming the fourth Toffees player to achieve the landmark after Tim Howard (354), Leon Osman (352) and David Unsworth (302).


Sunderland

Sunderland have won just one of their last eight league games (D2, L5).
However, they are aiming for consecutive Premier League away victories for the first time since May 2014 when they beat Chelsea and Manchester United under Gus Poyet.
On the day of this game, Sunderland will have spent 175 days in the relegation zone this season - more than any other Premier League side.
David Moyes hasn't named an unchanged line-up in any of his last 60 games in the Premier League since his penultimate game as Everton manager in May 2013.

TEAM NEWS

Sunderland's Papy Djilobodji returns from a ban but loanee Jason Denayer is ineligible against his parent club.

Jan Kirchhoff and Steven Pienaar are not yet fit to play, while Jack Rodwell is unlikely to feature because a hamstring problem.

Manchester City defenders Vincent Kompany and Aleksandar Kolarov are both in contention despite missing the win against Huddersfield through injury.

David Silva and Willy Caballero could return after being rested on Wednesday.

MATCH FACTS

Head-to-head

Manchester City have won their last three league and cup games at the Stadium of Light. Prior to that run, they had lost four consecutive games on Wearside (all 1-0 defeats).
Home and away, City have claimed six successive wins against Sunderland in all competitions (including a 4-1 win in the League Cup last season).


Sunderland

Sunderland have won just one of their last nine league games (D2, L6).
The Black Cats have failed to score in four of their last five top-flight matches - the exception being their 4-0 win at Crystal Palace.
David Moyes has only lost two of 12 Premier League home games against Manchester City in his managerial career (W7, D3).
Jermain Defoe has scored eight Premier League goals against Manchester City - the only side he has a better record against is Wigan (10 goals).
Defoe has attempted 64 shots in the Premier League this season - 40 more than any other Sunderland player.


Manchester City

City are chasing a fourth consecutive league victory, which would represent their best run since winning their opening six top-flight matches under Pep Guardiola.
They have won three of their last six Premier League away games, losing the other three.
Pep Guardiola has suffered five Premier League defeats as Manchester City boss this season - he's never lost six in a single league campaign as a manager.
Sergio Aguero has been involved in six goals in his last four Premier League appearances against Sunderland (four goals and two assists).

新特蘭前鋒養傷散心
點知變咗救人英雄

新特蘭前鋒鄧肯禾特摩亞早前因為十字韌帶受傷而提早收咧，於是同女友蘇菲走去巴巴多斯散心。點知，喺巴巴多斯期間竟然遇上撞船意外，而23歲嘅禾特摩亞仲一口氣救咗3名長者乘客上水㖭。其中一名獲救嘅乘客事後對禾特摩亞讚不絕口：「事發時真係好恐怖，我可以拾番條命真係好彩。禾特摩亞奮不顧身咁幫所有人上水，佢真係好人。」

係比季初進步嘅 但無乜機會畀佢發揮

Watford will make a late decision on whether to start Troy Deeney, who has returned to training following illness.

Sebastian Prodl is also doubtful with a calf problem, while Valon Behrami has been ruled out with a thigh injury.

Sunderland midfielder Lee Cattermole is getting closer to making his first appearance since September after a hip problem but Saturday may come too soon.

Victor Anichebe has also returned to training after a long-term injury, while Lamine Kone is fit again.


MATCH FACTS

Head-to-head

The last three league matches between these sides at Vicarage Road have ended in draws.
Watford's only win in their last nine league meetings with Sunderland came on their trip to the Stadium of Light in December 2015 (D3, L5).
Watford

Watford have only won two of their previous 13 league matches (D4, L7) and they have gone four games without a victory.
They have kept just one clean sheet in their last 18 Premier League fixtures.
A victory for the Hornets would ensure they equal their best tally of six Premier League home wins in a season, which they set last year.
Top scorer Troy Deeney could become the first Watford player to score in four consecutive Premier League home matches.
Sunderland

Sunderland's only win in 11 league matches was by a 4-0 scoreline at Crystal Palace on 4 February (D3, L7).
They have failed to score in six of their last seven Premier League games.
Sunderland could go five consecutive top-flight matches without a goal for the first time since February 1982.
David Moyes' side have scored a league-low two goals in the opening 30 minutes of their games this season.
Jermain Defoe has gone four league games without a goal since scoring six in the previous six matches.
